recipes.title: 96 Calorie Protein Cakey Frosted Cosmic Brownie
recipes.description: A high-protein, low-calorie cakey brownie topped with a creamy chocolate frosting and optional confetti chips. Each brownie is under 100 calories and packed with protein thanks to pumpkin, egg whites, and protein powder.
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine the wet ingredients: almond milk, egg whites, and canned pumpkin. Mix well.
In a separate mixing bowl, combine the dry ingredients: all-purpose flour, granulated sugar substitute, protein powder, cocoa powder, baking soda, and baking powder. Mix well.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix thoroughly until fully combined, making sure to avoid any clumping. Mix again to ensure a smooth batter.
Spray a large square or rectangular baking dish with cooking spray, or line it with parchment paper.
Pour the brownie batter into the prepared baking dish and spread evenly.
Bake at 350°F for 35 minutes. Remove slightly underdone as they will continue to cook out of the oven.
While the brownies are baking, combine the frosting ingredients in a mixing bowl: Greek yogurt, confectioners sugar substitute, and cocoa powder. Mix until smooth, then refrigerate for 10 minutes.
Once brownies are done and have cooled slightly, spread the chilled frosting evenly over the top. Optionally, sprinkle with confetti or candy chips.
For a firmer frosting, place the frosted brownies in the fridge for an additional 10+ minutes.
Slice into a 3x3 grid to yield 9 brownies. Serve and enjoy!
